relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: After the flood, the lines of Noah’s sons began to unfold into peoples and lands.
Japheth’s line spread through many names, opening one branch of the renewed earth.
Gomer’s descendants continued the branching of families.
Javan’s line moved toward coastlands and distant peoples.
From these came peoples of coastlands, each with land, language, family, and nation.
Ham’s line unfolded through Cush, Mizraim, Put, and Canaan.
Cush’s descendants spread into further houses and names.
From Cush came Nimrod, a mighty one upon the earth.
Nimrod’s power became proverbial, a hunter before the Eternal, wrapped in ambiguity.
His kingdom began in Babel and Shinar, the first shadow of empire rising before the tower story.
From that land came city-building, power gathering itself into places of name.
Great cities rose, carrying human organization and ambition forward.
Mizraim’s line branched through many peoples.
From these lines came peoples known later in Israel’s story.
Canaan’s line began, already carrying forward the tension from Noah’s household.
Canaanite peoples multiplied by name.
More Canaanite houses appeared in the spread of nations.
The families of Canaan spread abroad.
Their boundaries were named, rooting the genealogy in land and future conflict.
These were Ham’s sons by family, tongue, land, and nation.
Shem’s line opened, ancestor of Eber’s children and bearer of a later covenantal thread.
Shem’s sons became another branch of the nations.
Aram’s line unfolded through named descendants.
Through Arpachshad and Shelah came Eber.
In Peleg’s days the earth was divided, and the theme of scattering drew near.
Joktan’s line began to unfold.
More names carried the line outward.
The genealogy continued through further branches.
Joktan’s sons completed this branch of the line.
Their dwelling stretched toward the east.
Shem’s descendants were named by family, tongue, land, and nation.
From Noah’s sons the nations spread over the earth after the flood, many languages and lands emerging from one preserved family.
